response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile engineer with a genuine performance-systems background, but the fit for this role is poor. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014–2015, and C++ does not even appear in his skills list; his career since has centered on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms and DLang services. The role's core requirements — sustained C++/STL development, MQTT/REST for IoT, message-broker architectures, and IEC 62443/SDL industrial-cybersecurity practice — are largely absent. His low-latency, multi-threaded DLang work and craftsmanship culture are transferable, but not enough to offset the missing core stack and domain.
strengths: Real high-performance systems experience: low-latency, asynchronous, high-throughput services handling millions of requests per second
Strong engineering culture — TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source maintainership
Broad polyglot background (systems and web) with an MIT computer science degree
concerns: Professional C++ exposure is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014–2015); C++ is absent from his stated skills
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus), or IEC 62443/SDL security experience
No message-broker or Azure experience; recent trajectory is web/full-stack, diverging from this role's direction

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IoT domain background
